name: Mondoñedo - Trabada Return
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Mondoñedo traverse a diverse landscape in the heart of Galicia, Spain. The region features a mix of terrain, including hilly profiles and significant elevation gains, without excessively steep climbs. Cyclists will find routes winding through the Mondoñedo Valley, nestled between the Sierras de Lourenzá and A Toxiza, characterized by green forests and numerous rivers. The area offers well-paved paths suitable for road bikes, catering to various skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Mondoñedo The most popular road cycling route is Mondoñedo and Serra do Xistal Loop, a 51.0 miles (82.1 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 41 minutes to complete. This route features substantial elevation gain through the scenic Serra do Xistal. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Mondoñedo - Trabada Return, a difficult 28.8 miles (46.3 km) path. This route offers a challenging ride with considerable climbing through the Galician countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Mondoñedo Cathedral – Foz Harbor loop from Mondoñedo, a 46.2 miles (74.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain towards the coast, often completed in about 4 hours 3 minutes. Road cycling around Mondoñedo is defined by green valleys, forested hills, and river systems, with routes offering both moderate and difficult challenges. The network provides options for different ability levels, from shorter loops to longer, more demanding rides. The routes in Mondoñedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mondoñedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Mondoñedo?
A: Mondoñedo offers a selection of road cycling routes, with over 10 routes available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, including moderate and difficult options, allowing cyclists to explore the diverse Galician landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Mondoñedo?
A: The region of Mondoñedo is characterized by its green landscapes year-round, making it suitable for road cycling across many seasons. However, to enjoy the most pleasant weather conditions and avoid extreme heat or heavy rain, spring and early autumn are generally ideal for exploring the routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Mondoñedo's road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Mondoñedo feature a diverse mix of terrain. You can expect hilly profiles with significant elevation gains, but generally without excessively steep climbs, making them suitable for road bikes. Routes traverse the Mondoñedo Valley, nestled between mountain ranges, offering a blend of green forests, river systems, and well-paved paths.

Q: Are there any notable natural attractions to see while cycling in Mondoñedo?
A: Yes, the Mondoñedo region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter numerous streams and rivers, including the Masma River. A notable natural spectacle is the Salto Fervenza waterfall. Additionally, the area is home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto do Coro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tronceda River Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored near some routes. The Cova do Rei Cintolo, Galicia's largest cave, is also a significant natural landmark in the area.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore on a road cycling trip around Mondoñedo?
A: Mondoñedo boasts a rich historical heritage. The city's old quarter is a Historic-Artistic Site, centered around the impressive 13th-century Mondoñedo Cathedral. You might also pass by the historic Monastery of San Salvador. The ancient Fonte Vella, built in 1548, and the charming Os Muiños district with its old bridges, offer further cultural enrichment along your rides.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, from elevated points within the region, such as Padornelo, cyclists can enjoy expansive panoramic views of Mondoñedo city and the surrounding valley. Many routes also offer picturesque vistas of the green, forested landscapes and river systems characteristic of the area.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Mondoñedo?
A: While many routes in Mondoñedo involve significant elevation, there are moderate options that might be suitable for families with some cycling experience. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40321614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo Cathedral – A Rapadoira Beach loop from Mondoñedo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 50.5 km route with manageable elevation, offering a scenic ride towards the coast.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Mondoñedo?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Mondoñedo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942857386/mondonedo-and-serra-do-xistal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo and Serra do Xistal Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38183458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo Cathedral – Foz Harbor loop from Mondoñedo&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you towards the coast.

Q: What do other road cyclists appreciate about the routes in Mondoñedo?
A: The road cycling routes in Mondoñedo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging climbs, and the rewarding Galician scenery, including green valleys, forested hills, and river systems.

Q: Are there routes that combine coastal views with inland scenery?
A: Absolutely. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38183458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo Cathedral – Foz Harbor loop from Mondoñedo&lt;/a&gt; offer a fantastic blend of inland Galician landscapes and coastal views as you approach the sea. This particular route covers 74.4 km and provides varied terrain.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for advanced road cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: Mondoñedo is an excellent destination for advanced road cyclists. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942857386/mondonedo-and-serra-do-xistal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo and Serra do Xistal Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an 82.1 km difficult trail with significant elevation gain, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942878370/mondonedo-trabada-return" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo - Trabada Return&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 46.3 km ride, provide substantial climbs and rewarding experiences for experienced riders.

Q: Can I find accommodation in Mondoñedo that caters to cyclists?
A: Mondoñedo is a favored stopping point on the Camino del Norte, a pilgrimage route, which means the city is accustomed to welcoming travelers, including cyclists. You can find various accommodation options, from hotels to guesthouses, many of which are likely to offer facilities or services beneficial to cyclists, such as secure bike storage.
tips: Category 2 ascent, 6.11 km with a positive elevation gain of 494 m with an average elevation gain of 8.1% up to 648 m

The Cathedral Basilica of the Virgin of the Assumption of Mondoñedo is one of the episcopal sees of the diocese of Mondoñedo-Ferrol in Galicia. The temple, in the Romanesque style with later additions and reforms, also has contributions from the Baroque and Gothic styles. Although its construction dates from the 13th century, the cathedral was not completed until the 14th century. Later, during the Middle Ages, a choir, the ambulatory and the sacristy were built, while the cloister with a square plan with semicircular arches and Doric columns dates from the 17th century.
